# flag-prune

> Remove completed feature flags safely from JavaScript and TypeScript.

## Quick start

Preview a change:

```sh
npx flag-prune --set 'useFlag("new-checkout")=true' ./src
```

Review the diff, then write it:

```sh
npx flag-prune --set 'useFlag("new-checkout")=true' --write ./src
```

Run your project's typecheck, lint, dead-code elimination, and tests after writing the change.

## Recommended workflow

flag-prune is the deterministic step in a larger cleanup:

1. flag-prune (dry-run, review, then --write; add --flatten-blocks to de-scope)
2. typecheck, lint, format, test
3. dead-code removal tool (Knip, Fallow, ts-prune)
4. optional LLM pass for readability and scoping refactors

See [Recommended workflow](https://github.com/Tymek/flag-prune/blob/master/docs/workflow.md)
for the full pipeline, an LLM cleanup prompt, and examples.

Run `npx flag-prune --help` to see all parameters, or open [full documentation](https://github.com/Tymek/flag-prune/blob/master/docs/README.md)
